analysis.doc: Synchronized from Linear issue identifier: POL-277

POL-277.title: FIX: Fail packet generation when impl child has empty allowed_scope or placeholder goal
POL-277.description:
## Context

PR 72 (polaris-run-[POL-268](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-268/implement-polaris-init-and-repository-adoption-workflow)-2026-06-01-001) produced 16 empty commits across 16 children. Every packet had `allowed_scope: []` and `validation_commands: []`. The primary_goal was a placeholder ("Implement POL-XXX: 'POL-XXX'") with no Goal, Scope, Acceptance Criteria, or Validation content extracted from the issue body.

Workers received non-actionable packets and completed in <100ms with zero files changed.

## Root cause

The packet generator does not validate packet quality before writing packets to disk or dispatching workers. It generates packets from Linear issue metadata even when the issue body contains no implementation scope.

## Goal

Add a hard gate in the packet generator (and/or the CLI dispatch path) that refuses to emit an `impl` worker packet when:

1. `allowed_scope` is empty (`[]`)
2. `primary_goal` is a placeholder (issue title repeated verbatim, with no extractable goal body)
3. Both `validation_commands` is empty AND `validation_waiver` is absent

## Scope

* `src/loop/` ŌĆö packet generation and dispatch path
* `src/cli/` ŌĆö dispatch CLI command if gate belongs there

## Acceptance Criteria

* `npm run polaris -- loop dispatch` exits non-zero and emits `packet-generation-failed` telemetry when scope is empty
* Error message names the specific missing field and the child issue ID
* Existing tests for well-formed packets continue to pass
* New test: dispatch with an issue that has no scope body ŌåÆ should fail with clear error

## Validation

```
npm test -- --grep "packet.*empty.*scope|empty.*allowed_scope"
npm run build
```

## Ordering

This is the highest-priority fix. All other guardrails are defense-in-depth against a packet that never should have been generated.
key_requirements: Add a hard gate in the packet generator (and/or the CLI dispatch path) that refuses to emit an `impl` worker packet when:
`allowed_scope` is empty (`[]`)
`primary_goal` is a placeholder (issue title repeated verbatim, with no extractable goal body)
Both `validation_commands` is empty AND `validation_waiver` is absent
`npm run polaris -- loop dispatch` exits non-zero and emits `packet-generation-failed` telemetry when scope is empty
Error message names the specific missing field and the child issue ID
Existing tests for well-formed packets continue to pass
New test: dispatch with an issue that has no scope body ŌåÆ should fail with clear error
allowed_scope: `src/loop/` ŌĆö packet generation and dispatch path
`src/cli/` ŌĆö dispatch CLI command if gate belongs there

POL-268.title: IMPLEMENT: Polaris init and repository adoption workflow
POL-268.description:
## Objective

Implement the `polaris init` and `polaris init --adopt` workflows as defined by the analysis in [POL-257](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-257/analyze-define-polaris-init-and-repository-adoption-workflow).

## Scope

Extends `src/cli/init.ts` with two distinct flows:

* `polaris init` ŌĆö new/empty repo initialization (scaffold, SmartDocs structure, atlas baseline)
* `polaris init --adopt` ŌĆö existing repo adoption (Phase A: config lock + scan ŌåÆ Phase B: plan + approval ŌåÆ Phase C: migration, cognition, instruction refactor, atlas, stage)

## Analysis reference

See [POL-257](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-257/analyze-define-polaris-init-and-repository-adoption-workflow) and `smartdocs/raw/pol-257-init-adoption-workflow-analysis.md` for the full order-of-operations, schemas, and handling rules.

## Children

| \# | Issue | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 1 | [POL-258](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-258/impl-add-repo-state-detection-to-polaris-init) | Repo state detection |
| 2 | [POL-259](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-259/impl-add-read-only-repo-scanner-reposcaninventory) | Read-only repo scanner (RepoScanInventory) |
| 3 | [POL-260](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-260/impl-add-adoption-plan-generator-adoptionplan) | Adoption plan generator (AdoptionPlan) |
| 4 | [POL-261](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-261/impl-add-user-approval-gate-to-polaris-init-adopt) | User approval gate |
| 5 | [POL-262](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-262/impl-add-minimal-provider-config-lock-for-polaris-init-adopt) | Minimal provider config lock |
| 6 | [POL-263](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-263/impl-add-smartdocs-migration-step-to-polaris-init-adopt) | SmartDocs migration step |
| 7 | [POL-264](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-264/impl-add-folder-cognition-generation-to-polaris-init-adopt) | Folder cognition generation |
| 8 | [POL-265](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-265/impl-add-agent-instruction-file-handler-to-polaris-init-adopt) | Agent instruction file handler |
| 9 | [POL-266](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-266/impl-integrate-atlas-generation-into-polaris-init-adopt) | Atlas generation integration |
| 10 | [POL-267](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-267/impl-add-adoption-stagecommit-finalization-to-polaris-init-adopt) | Stage/commit finalization |

## Execution order

[POL-258](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-258/impl-add-repo-state-detection-to-polaris-init) ŌåÆ ([POL-259](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-259/impl-add-read-only-repo-scanner-reposcaninventory), [POL-262](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-262/impl-add-minimal-provider-config-lock-for-polaris-init-adopt) in parallel) ŌåÆ [POL-260](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-260/impl-add-adoption-plan-generator-adoptionplan) ŌåÆ [POL-261](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-261/impl-add-user-approval-gate-to-polaris-init-adopt) ŌåÆ ([POL-263](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-263/impl-add-smartdocs-migration-step-to-polaris-init-adopt), [POL-265](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-265/impl-add-agent-instruction-file-handler-to-polaris-init-adopt) in parallel) ŌåÆ [POL-264](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-264/impl-add-folder-cognition-generation-to-polaris-init-adopt) ŌåÆ [POL-266](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-266/impl-integrate-atlas-generation-into-polaris-init-adopt) ŌåÆ [POL-267](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-267/impl-add-adoption-stagecommit-finalization-to-polaris-init-adopt)
key_requirements: Implement the `polaris init` and `polaris init --adopt` workflows as defined by the analysis in [POL-257](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-257/analyze-define-polaris-init-and-repository-adoption-workflow).
allowed_scope: Extends `src/cli/init.ts` with two distinct flows:
`polaris init` ŌĆö new/empty repo initialization (scaffold, SmartDocs structure, atlas baseline)
`polaris init --adopt` ŌĆö existing repo adoption (Phase A: config lock + scan ŌåÆ Phase B: plan + approval ŌåÆ Phase C: migration, cognition, instruction refactor, atlas, stage)

POL-279.title: FIX: polaris finalize must verify non-artifact commit diff per child before creating PR
POL-279.description:
## Context

`polaris finalize` for polaris-run-[POL-268](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-268/implement-polaris-init-and-repository-adoption-workflow)-2026-06-01-001 created PR 72 with 16 "completed" children, every one of which had a commit containing zero changed files. Finalize read `current-state-pol-268-reset.json` which showed `completed_children: [all 16]` and `status: cluster-complete`, then created the PR without inspecting the actual git diff.

The `cluster-state.json` (the cluster's own state surface) showed all children as `"status": "dispatched"` with empty `result_pointers`, `commits`, and `validation_results` ŌĆö a direct contradiction ŌĆö but finalize did not cross-check these.

## Goal

Finalize must verify that each child in `completed_children` satisfies ALL of:

1. A commit hash is recorded (either in `cluster-state.commits` or the result file)
2. `git show <hash> --stat` returns at least one file that is NOT an artifact path (`.polaris/clusters/`, `.taskchain_artifacts/`, `.polaris/runs/`)
3. Either `validation_commands` ran and passed, OR the packet carries an explicit `validation_waiver`

If a child fails any check, finalize must halt with:

```
Blocking finalize: child POL-XXX has no non-artifact commit evidence.
Resolve: close PR if artifact-only, or re-run child with real implementation.
```

Artifact-only children are allowed only when the packet explicitly sets `artifact_only: true` (set by the foreman at analysis time, not by the worker).

## Scope

* `src/cli/` ŌĆö `polaris finalize` CLI command
* `src/loop/` ŌĆö finalize support logic if applicable

## Acceptance Criteria

* Finalize with all-empty-commit children ŌåÆ exits non-zero, lists each failing child
* Finalize with one artifact-only child carrying `artifact_only: true` in packet ŌåÆ passes for that child
* Finalize with a real implementation child (files changed, validation passed) ŌåÆ continues normally
* New test covering the artifact-only rejection path

## Validation

```
npm test -- --grep "finalize|artifact.*only"
npm run build
```

## Ordering

Must be implemented alongside or after [POL-277](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-277/fix-fail-packet-generation-when-impl-child-has-empty-allowed-scope-or) (scope gate). Defense-in-depth for the case where a packet escapes the scope gate and workers produce empty commits.
key_requirements: Finalize must verify that each child in `completed_children` satisfies ALL of:
A commit hash is recorded (either in `cluster-state.commits` or the result file)
`git show <hash> --stat` returns at least one file that is NOT an artifact path (`.polaris/clusters/`, `.taskchain_artifacts/`, `.polaris/runs/`)
Either `validation_commands` ran and passed, OR the packet carries an explicit `validation_waiver`
If a child fails any check, finalize must halt with:
Blocking finalize: child POL-XXX has no non-artifact commit evidence.
Resolve: close PR if artifact-only, or re-run child with real implementation.
Artifact-only children are allowed only when the packet explicitly sets `artifact_only: true` (set by the foreman at analysis time, not by the worker).
Finalize with all-empty-commit children ŌåÆ exits non-zero, lists each failing child
Finalize with one artifact-only child carrying `artifact_only: true` in packet ŌåÆ passes for that child
Finalize with a real implementation child (files changed, validation passed) ŌåÆ continues normally
New test covering the artifact-only rejection path
allowed_scope: `src/cli/` ŌĆö `polaris finalize` CLI command
`src/loop/` ŌĆö finalize support logic if applicable

POL-278.title: FIX: Extend empty-scope guard from agent-subtask adapter to terminal-cli adapter
POL-278.description:
## Context

`src/loop/adapters/agent-subtask.ts` (current working tree) has an empty-scope guard added after PR 72:

```typescript
if (isWorkerPacket(packet) && packet.worker_role === 'impl') {
  const allowed = Array.isArray(packet.instructions?.allowed_scope) ? packet.instructions.allowed_scope : [];
  if (allowed.length === 0) {
    // returns blocked result, exit_code: 1
  }
}
```

The `terminal-cli` adapter (used for Copilot workers in PR 72) has no equivalent guard. All 15 Copilot-dispatched children in PR 72 passed through terminal-cli with empty-scope packets and completed in <100ms.

## Goal

Add the same empty-scope guard to the terminal-cli adapter so that impl packets with empty `allowed_scope` are rejected at the adapter level regardless of provider.

Consider extracting the guard into a shared `validateWorkerPacket()` function in `src/loop/adapters/` so both adapters (and any future adapters) use the same validation.

## Scope

* `src/loop/adapters/terminal-cli.ts` ŌĆö add guard
* `src/loop/adapters/agent-subtask.ts` ŌĆö optionally extract shared validator
* `src/loop/adapters/` ŌĆö optional shared validator module

## Acceptance Criteria

* Terminal-cli adapter returns blocked result when `allowed_scope` is empty for impl packet
* Agent-subtask adapter retains existing guard behavior
* If shared validator extracted: both adapters delegate to it
* New adapter test: empty-scope impl packet via terminal-cli ŌåÆ blocked, exit_code: 1

## Validation

```
npm test -- --grep "terminal-cli|adapter.*scope"
npm run build
```

## Ordering

Blocked by [POL-277](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-277/fix-fail-packet-generation-when-impl-child-has-empty-allowed-scope-or) (conceptually ŌĆö packet should never reach adapter with empty scope, but this is defense-in-depth for any adapter that receives such a packet).
key_requirements: Add the same empty-scope guard to the terminal-cli adapter so that impl packets with empty `allowed_scope` are rejected at the adapter level regardless of provider.
Consider extracting the guard into a shared `validateWorkerPacket()` function in `src/loop/adapters/` so both adapters (and any future adapters) use the same validation.
Terminal-cli adapter returns blocked result when `allowed_scope` is empty for impl packet
Agent-subtask adapter retains existing guard behavior
If shared validator extracted: both adapters delegate to it
New adapter test: empty-scope impl packet via terminal-cli ŌåÆ blocked, exit_code: 1
allowed_scope: `src/loop/adapters/terminal-cli.ts` ŌĆö add guard
`src/loop/adapters/agent-subtask.ts` ŌĆö optionally extract shared validator
`src/loop/adapters/` ŌĆö optional shared validator module

POL-260.title: IMPL: Add adoption plan generator (AdoptionPlan)
key_requirements: Implement a plan generator that consumes a `RepoScanInventory` and produces a structured `AdoptionPlan` ŌĆö both as JSON (`.polaris/adoption-plan.json`) and human-readable Markdown (`.polaris/adoption-plan.md`).
allowed_scope: Create `src/cli/adopt-plan.ts` implementing `generateAdoptionPlan(inventory: RepoScanInventory): AdoptionPlan`.

POL-257.title: ANALYZE: Define Polaris init and repository adoption workflow

POL-263.title: IMPL: Add SmartDocs migration step to polaris init --adopt
POL-263.description:
## Objective

Implement Phase C Step C2: move discovered SmartDocs candidates to `smartdocs/raw/` and record provenance.

## Scope

Create `src/cli/adopt-smartdocs.ts` implementing `migrateSmartDocs(plan: AdoptionPlan): Promise<void>`.

### Migration rules

* For each `AdoptionStep` in the plan with `category: "smartdocs-migrate"` and `status: "pending"`:
  1. Attempt `git mv <source_path> smartdocs/raw/<basename>` to preserve git history
  2. If `git mv` fails (e.g. not in a git repo, or conflict), fallback to `fs.rename()` + log the fallback
  3. Mark the step `status: "completed"` in `.polaris/adoption-plan.json`
* Write provenance record to `.polaris/adoption-provenance.json`:

  ```json
  [{ "old_path": "docs/arch.md", "new_path": "smartdocs/raw/arch.md", "moved_at": "...", "method": "git-mv" }]
  ```

### What NOT to move (even if detected as candidate)

* `README.md` at repo root
* `CHANGELOG.md`, `LICENSE`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`
* Files in `test/`, `fixtures/`, `__mocks__/`
* Files already in `smartdocs/`
* Generated docs (typedoc/jsdoc output)

### Resumability

On re-run, skip steps already `status: "completed"` in `.polaris/adoption-plan.json`. Do not re-move already-moved files.

## Files to touch

* `src/cli/adopt-smartdocs.ts` (new)
* `src/cli/init.ts` ŌĆö wire into Phase C after `.polaris/` scaffold creation

## Depends on

[POL-257](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-257/analyze-define-polaris-init-and-repository-adoption-workflow) Child 4 (approval gate ŌĆö migration only runs after approval).
key_requirements: Implement Phase C Step C2: move discovered SmartDocs candidates to `smartdocs/raw/` and record provenance.
allowed_scope: Create `src/cli/adopt-smartdocs.ts` implementing `migrateSmartDocs(plan: AdoptionPlan): Promise<void>`.

POL-265.title: IMPL: Add agent instruction file handler to polaris init --adopt
POL-265.description:
## Objective

Implement Phase C Step C4: detect existing agent instruction files (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, etc.) and apply preserve/migrate/thin-adapter logic without silently deleting content.

## Scope

Create `src/cli/adopt-instructions.ts` implementing `handleInstructionFiles(plan: AdoptionPlan, inventory: RepoScanInventory): Promise<void>`.

### Files to detect

* `CLAUDE.md` (Claude Code)
* `AGENTS.md` (OpenAI Agents SDK)
* `.github/copilot-instructions.md` (GitHub Copilot)
* `.cursorrules` or `.cursor/rules/**/*.md` (Cursor)
* `.aider.conf.yml` or `AIDER.md` (Aider)
* `**/GEMINI.md` (Gemini)

### Decision rules

| Condition | Action |
| -- | -- |
| File already has `<!-- polaris:delegate -->` marker or references POLARIS.md | `preserve` |
| No Polaris doctrine exists in this repo yet | `preserve` |
| Polaris doctrine exists, file < 500 bytes with generic content | `thin-adapter` |
| Polaris doctrine exists, file Ōēź 500 bytes with repo-specific content | `migrate` |

### Thin adapter content

```markdown
<!-- polaris:adapter ŌĆö generated by polaris init --adopt -->
<!-- Original content preserved in smartdocs/raw/migrated-instructions/<filename> -->

This repository uses Polaris. For agent instructions, see the route-local POLARIS.md file
in the folder you are working in. For global runtime behavior, see the root POLARIS.md.
```

### Migrate action

1. Copy original content to `smartdocs/raw/migrated-instructions/<filename>`
2. Replace file content with thin adapter
3. Record in adoption provenance

### Never delete

Never `rm` or truncate an instruction file. The `migrate` action copies first, then replaces. If the copy fails, abort the step and report.

## Files to touch

* `src/cli/adopt-instructions.ts` (new)
* `src/cli/init.ts` ŌĆö wire into Phase C after SmartDocs migration (C2), before atlas generation (C5)

## Depends on

[POL-257](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-257/analyze-define-polaris-init-and-repository-adoption-workflow) Child 4 (approval gate).
key_requirements: Implement Phase C Step C4: detect existing agent instruction files (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, etc.) and apply preserve/migrate/thin-adapter logic without silently deleting content.
allowed_scope: Create `src/cli/adopt-instructions.ts` implementing `handleInstructionFiles(plan: AdoptionPlan, inventory: RepoScanInventory): Promise<void>`.

POL-266.title: IMPL: Integrate atlas generation into polaris init --adopt
POL-266.description:
## Objective

Implement Phase C Step C5: run `polaris map index` as the final pre-stage step of adoption and record the baseline coverage.

## Scope

Add `runAdoptionAtlas(plan: AdoptionPlan): Promise<void>` in `src/cli/init.ts` or `src/cli/adopt-atlas.ts`.

### Invocation

Call the existing `runMapIndex()` from `src/map/index.ts` with:

* `seedCognition: false` ŌĆö cognition files were already created in C3; do not overwrite
* No `autoWriteAbove` threshold enforcement ŌĆö index all files regardless of confidence

### Baseline coverage

After indexing completes, read `.polaris/map/index.json` and write `adoption_baseline_coverage_pct` to it:

```json
{
  "scan_date": "...",
  "file_count": 142,
  "coverage_pct": 61,
  "adoption_baseline_coverage_pct": 61,
  ...
}
```

This field marks the starting point. Future `polaris map validate` runs can measure improvement from this baseline.

### Validation

After indexing:

* Verify that at least one file from `smartdocs/raw/` appears in the atlas (confirms moved docs are discoverable)
* Log a warning (not an error) if `needs-review.json` has > 20% of total files (adoption debt)

## Files to touch

* `src/cli/adopt-atlas.ts` (new) or inline in `src/cli/init.ts`
* `src/map/index.ts` ŌĆö add `seedCognition` and `skipThreshold` options if not already present

## Depends on

[POL-257](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-257/analyze-define-polaris-init-and-repository-adoption-workflow) Child 7 (cognition generation) and Child 8 (instruction file handler) ŌĆö both must complete before atlas so the walk-up instruction resolution is correct.
key_requirements: Implement Phase C Step C5: run `polaris map index` as the final pre-stage step of adoption and record the baseline coverage.
allowed_scope: Add `runAdoptionAtlas(plan: AdoptionPlan): Promise<void>` in `src/cli/init.ts` or `src/cli/adopt-atlas.ts`.
validation_commands: Verify that at least one file from `smartdocs/raw/` appears in the atlas (confirms moved docs are discoverable)
Log a warning (not an error) if `needs-review.json` has > 20% of total files (adoption debt)

POL-262.title: IMPL: Add minimal provider config lock for polaris init --adopt
POL-262.description:
## Objective

Write `polaris.config.json` with dispatch-safe defaults as the very first write in the `polaris init --adopt` flow (Phase A, Step A1), before the repo scan runs. This is the only permitted write in Phase A.

## Scope

Extend `src/cli/init.ts` to write a locked `polaris.config.json` before calling `scanRepo()`.

### Required locked config values

```json
{
  "execution": {
    "rotation": [],
    "allowCrossAgentFallback": false,
    "adapter": "terminal-cli"
  },
  "orchestration": {
    "mode": "supervised"
  }
}
```

These values must be written even if a `polaris.config.json` already partially exists (merge with existing, but the locked keys always win for the adoption session).

### Integration with [POL-256](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-256/analyze-define-provider-role-config-fallback-and-dispatch-governance)

Do not define provider roles here. Provider role config belongs to [POL-256](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-256/analyze-define-provider-role-config-fallback-and-dispatch-governance). This step only disables cross-agent dispatch. When [POL-256](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-256/analyze-define-provider-role-config-fallback-and-dispatch-governance) ships provider role config, the adoption lock should reference those constraints rather than hard-coding them here.

### Idempotency

If `polaris.config.json` already contains the locked values, skip the write and log `"Provider config already locked ŌĆö skipping."` (for resume path).

## Files to touch

* `src/cli/init.ts` ŌĆö add `writeAdoptionConfigLock()` call before `scanRepo()`
* `src/config/loader.ts` ŌĆö verify deep-merge correctly handles the locked keys

## Acceptance criteria

* `polaris.config.json` is written before any scan or plan generation
* Locked keys cannot be overridden by a pre-existing partial config during the adoption session
* Idempotent on resume

## Depends on

[POL-257](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-257/analyze-define-polaris-init-and-repository-adoption-workflow) Child 1 (state detection). Coordinate with [POL-256](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-256/analyze-define-provider-role-config-fallback-and-dispatch-governance) analysis for provider role hook point.
key_requirements: Write `polaris.config.json` with dispatch-safe defaults as the very first write in the `polaris init --adopt` flow (Phase A, Step A1), before the repo scan runs. This is the only permitted write in Phase A.
`polaris.config.json` is written before any scan or plan generation
Locked keys cannot be overridden by a pre-existing partial config during the adoption session
Idempotent on resume
allowed_scope: Extend `src/cli/init.ts` to write a locked `polaris.config.json` before calling `scanRepo()`.

POL-264.title: IMPL: Add folder cognition generation to polaris init --adopt
POL-264.description:
## Objective

Implement Phase C Step C3: create draft `POLARIS.md` and `SUMMARY.md` templates for stable canonical folders in the adopted repo.

## Scope

Create `src/cli/adopt-cognition.ts` implementing `generateFolderCognition(plan: AdoptionPlan, inventory: RepoScanInventory): Promise<void>`.

### Inclusion rules

Create cognition files for each folder in `inventory.likely_canonical_folders` that:

* Is not in `generated_roots`, `cache_roots`, `fixture_roots`
* Does not contain `node_modules`, `dist`, `.git`, `.polaris/runs`, `.polaris/bootstrap` in its path
* Has Ōēź 3 source files
* Does not already have a `POLARIS.md`

### Template format

```markdown
<!-- polaris:draft -->
# <folder-name>

> Polaris draft ŌĆö review and remove the `<!-- polaris:draft -->` marker to promote.

## Purpose

<!-- One paragraph describing what this folder does. -->

**Domain:** unknown
**Route:** <folder-path>
**Taskchain:** unknown

## What belongs here

<!-- ... -->

## What does not belong here

<!-- ... -->
```

### Alignment with [POL-233](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-233/analyze-normalize-smartdocs-routing-folder-cognition-yaml-links-and) and [POL-241](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-241/analyze-define-folder-local-work-note-staging-and-librarian)

* All generated files are marked `<!-- polaris:draft -->` and are not treated as canonical until promoted
* Do not create cognition files in the `smartdocs/` subtree (those follow the SmartDocs lifecycle, not this step)

### Pass `--seed-cognition=false` to subsequent map index

Cognition files created here must not be overwritten when `polaris map index` runs in Step C5.

## Files to touch

* `src/cli/adopt-cognition.ts` (new)
* `src/cli/init.ts` ŌĆö wire into Phase C after SmartDocs migration (C2)

## Depends on

[POL-257](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-257/analyze-define-polaris-init-and-repository-adoption-workflow) Child 6 (SmartDocs migration must run first ŌĆö moved docs change folder structure).
key_requirements: Implement Phase C Step C3: create draft `POLARIS.md` and `SUMMARY.md` templates for stable canonical folders in the adopted repo.
allowed_scope: Create `src/cli/adopt-cognition.ts` implementing `generateFolderCognition(plan: AdoptionPlan, inventory: RepoScanInventory): Promise<void>`.

POL-261.title: IMPL: Add user approval gate to polaris init --adopt
POL-261.description:
## Objective

Add an interactive approval gate that pauses the adoption flow after the plan is generated, displays the plan, and requires explicit user confirmation before Phase C mutation begins.

## Scope

Add `promptApproval(plan: AdoptionPlan): Promise<boolean>` in `src/cli/adopt-plan.ts` (or a small `src/cli/adopt-approve.ts`).

### Behavior

1. Print the `.polaris/adoption-plan.md` content to stdout
2. Prompt: `"Proceed with adoption? [y/N]"` (default: N)
3. Read one line from stdin
4. If `y`/`Y`: set `plan.approved = true`, `plan.approved_at = <now>`, write updated `.polaris/adoption-plan.json`, return `true`
5. If anything else: print `"Adoption aborted. polaris.config.json is already written and safe to keep."`, return `false`

### `--yes` flag

When `polaris init --adopt --yes` is passed, skip the prompt and treat as approved. Print a notice: `"--yes flag set; skipping approval prompt."` Log the bypass in telemetry.

### Resume path

On `polaris init --resume`, if `.polaris/adoption-plan.json` already has `approved: true`, skip the prompt and continue from the last incomplete step.

## Files to touch

* `src/cli/adopt-approve.ts` (new) or add to `adopt-plan.ts`
* `src/cli/init.ts` ŌĆö insert approval gate between plan generation and Phase C

## Acceptance criteria

* Default answer is N (adoption does not proceed on empty input)
* `--yes` flag works and is logged
* Approved state is persisted before Phase C begins

## Depends on

[POL-257](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-257/analyze-define-polaris-init-and-repository-adoption-workflow) Child 3 (adoption plan generator).
key_requirements: Add an interactive approval gate that pauses the adoption flow after the plan is generated, displays the plan, and requires explicit user confirmation before Phase C mutation begins.
Default answer is N (adoption does not proceed on empty input)
`--yes` flag works and is logged
Approved state is persisted before Phase C begins
allowed_scope: Add `promptApproval(plan: AdoptionPlan): Promise<boolean>` in `src/cli/adopt-plan.ts` (or a small `src/cli/adopt-approve.ts`).

POL-259.title: IMPL: Add read-only repo scanner (RepoScanInventory)
key_requirements: Implement a read-only repo scanner that produces a `RepoScanInventory` JSON file at `.polaris/adoption-inventory.json`. No files are moved or modified during the scan.
allowed_scope: Create `src/cli/adopt-scan.ts` implementing `scanRepo(): Promise<RepoScanInventory>`.

POL-258.title: IMPL: Add repo state detection to polaris init
POL-258.description:
## Objective

Extend `src/cli/init.ts` to detect the current repository's initialization state before any action is taken. Detection is strictly read-only.

## Scope

Add a `detectRepoState()` function in `src/cli/init.ts` (or a new `src/cli/init-detect.ts`) that returns one of five states:

| State | Detection Criteria |
| -- | -- |
| `empty` | Directory is empty or contains only `.git/` |
| `new` | Has `package.json` or basic source structure, no existing docs/agent files/Polaris config |
| `partial` | Has `polaris.config.json` but no `.polaris/map/file-routes.json` |
| `existing` | Has source roots, docs, and/or agent instruction files but no `polaris.config.json` |
| `polaris-enabled` | Has `polaris.config.json` AND `.polaris/map/file-routes.json` |

Detection order: `polaris-enabled` ŌåÆ `partial` ŌåÆ `existing` ŌåÆ `new` ŌåÆ `empty`.

## Files to touch

* `src/cli/init.ts` ŌĆö add `detectRepoState()`, call it at the top of `runInit()`
* If the state is `existing` and `--adopt` flag is not present, print: `"This repo has existing content. Run polaris init --adopt to begin adoption."` and exit cleanly.

## Acceptance criteria

* `detectRepoState()` is exported and returns a typed `RepoState` union
* All 5 states are covered by unit tests in `test/`
* No file writes occur during detection
* Existing `runInit()` behavior is unchanged for `empty`/`new` states

## Depends on

Nothing ŌĆö this is the foundation for all other adoption children.
key_requirements: Extend `src/cli/init.ts` to detect the current repository's initialization state before any action is taken. Detection is strictly read-only.
`detectRepoState()` is exported and returns a typed `RepoState` union
All 5 states are covered by unit tests in `test/`
No file writes occur during detection
Existing `runInit()` behavior is unchanged for `empty`/`new` states
allowed_scope: Add a `detectRepoState()` function in `src/cli/init.ts` (or a new `src/cli/init-detect.ts`) that returns one of five states:
| State | Detection Criteria |
| `empty` | Directory is empty or contains only `.git/` |
| `new` | Has `package.json` or basic source structure, no existing docs/agent files/Polaris config |
| `partial` | Has `polaris.config.json` but no `.polaris/map/file-routes.json` |
| `existing` | Has source roots, docs, and/or agent instruction files but no `polaris.config.json` |
| `polaris-enabled` | Has `polaris.config.json` AND `.polaris/map/file-routes.json` |
Detection order: `polaris-enabled` ŌåÆ `partial` ŌåÆ `existing` ŌåÆ `new` ŌåÆ `empty`.

POL-267.title: IMPL: Add adoption stage/commit finalization to polaris init --adopt
POL-267.description:
## Objective Implement Phase C Step C6: collect all adoption changes into a coherent staged set, append runtime-artifact paths to `.gitignore`, and optionally commit with `--commit` flag. ## Scope Add `finalizeAdoption(plan: AdoptionPlan): Promise` in `src/cli/init.ts` or `src/cli/adopt-finalize.ts`. ### .gitignore additions (always applied) Append to `.gitignore` if not already present: ``` # Polaris runtime artifacts ŌĆö do not commit .polaris/runs/ .polaris/bootstrap/ .polaris/clusters/ .polaris/session-type ``` Do not add `.polaris/map/` ŌĆö atlas sidecar files ARE committed. ### Staging Run `git add` for all adoption outputs: * `.polaris/adoption-plan.json` * `.polaris/adoption-inventory.json` * `.polaris/adoption-provenance.json` * `polaris.config.json` * `.polaris/map/` (atlas files) * All new `POLARIS.md` and `SUMMARY.md` files created in C3 * All files moved/modified in C2 and C4 * `.gitignore` (modified) Do NOT stage `.polaris/runs/`, `.polaris/bootstrap/`, `.polaris/clusters/`, `.polaris/session-type`. ### `--commit` flag If `--commit` is passed, create a git commit: ``` chore: adopt Polaris init ŌĆö files moved, cognition files generated ``` Otherwise, leave staged and print: `"Adoption complete. Changes are staged. Review with git diff --staged, then commit when ready."` ### Resumability Mark all AdoptionStep entries as `status: "completed"` in `.polaris/adoption-plan.json` before staging. On re-run of `polaris init --adopt`, if all steps are completed, print `"Adoption already complete."` and exit. ### Print adoption summary ``` Polaris adoption complete. Files moved to SmartDocs: Cognition files generated: Instruction files handled: Atlas coverage (baseline):

% Runtime artifacts excluded from commit: .polaris/runs/, .polaris/bootstrap/, .polaris/clusters/ ``` ## Files to touch * `src/cli/adopt-finalize.ts` (new) or inline in `src/cli/init.ts` ## Depends on [POL-257](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-257/analyze-define-polaris-init-and-repository-adoption-workflow) Child 9 (atlas generation must complete before staging).
key_requirements: Implement Phase C Step C6: collect all adoption changes into a coherent staged set, append runtime-artifact paths to `.gitignore`, and optionally commit with `--commit` flag.
allowed_scope: Add `finalizeAdoption(plan: AdoptionPlan): Promise<void>` in `src/cli/init.ts` or `src/cli/adopt-finalize.ts`.
